englishRequirements.waiverNotes: Georgia Tech requires international applicants to demonstrate English proficiency using a University System of Georgia-approved method. The reviewed Georgia Tech pages do not publish GT-specific minimum TOEFL or IELTS scores. Georgia Tech accepts Duolingo English Test, InitialView, and Vericant as supplementary materials, but they do not satisfy the USG requirement.

scholarshipNotes: Georgia Tech offers institutional scholarships, and Georgia residents may qualify for merit-based HOPE or Zell Miller scholarships. The Office of Scholarships and Financial Aid says scholarships are awarded annually and may be based on academic excellence, financial need, and major of study. International students are often not awarded institutional scholarships, though private and emergency options may exist.

testingRequirements.latestSatTestDateNote: Self-reported SAT scores are due by the round deadline: October 31 for EA1, November 17 for EA2, and January 23 for Regular Decision. Official scores are required before orientation if admitted and enrolled.
testingRequirements.latestActTestDateNote: Self-reported ACT scores are due by the round deadline: October 31 for EA1, November 17 for EA2, and January 23 for Regular Decision. Official scores are required before orientation if admitted and enrolled.
